mnemonics: SQLite on Android is CRUD: Create, Read, Update, Delete.
Remember 'SQL' in SQLite stands for Structured Query Language.
DB Helper is your 'Helper' for Database operations.
keyPoints: SQLite is a lightweight, embedded relational database management system.
It is integrated into Android and does not require a separate server process.
Data is stored in a private folder on the device, accessible only to the app.
commonMistakes: Not closing database connections and cursors, leading to memory leaks.
Performing database operations on the main UI thread, causing application not responding (ANR) errors.
Forgetting to update the database version when altering the schema, leading to migration issues.
